cases.title: Latitude 0° (The Equator)
cases.description: The most important line of Latitude. It divides the world into the Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
steps: Definition: The Equator is the line marked 0° Latitude.
Hemispheres: It divides the Earth into North and South.
In Africa: It crosses 7 countries: Uganda, Kenya, Somalia, DRC, Congo, Gabon, and Sao Tome.
Climate: Areas near this line are usually hot and wet throughout the year (Equatorial Climate).

cases.title: The Tropical Zone
cases.description: The area between the Tropic of Cancer and the Tropic of Capricorn.
steps: Tropic of Cancer (23½°N): Passes through North Africa (Egypt, Libya, Algeria).
Tropic of Capricorn (23½°S): Passes through South Africa, Botswana, Namibia, Mozambique.
The Tropics: The shaded yellow area is the hottest part of the world.
Africa's Position: Africa is the only continent crossed by both tropics and the Equator.

cases.title: Longitude 0° (Greenwich)
cases.description: The main line of Longitude. It helps us calculate time.
steps: Definition: Longitudes run from North to South (Vertical).
The Prime Meridian: It is marked 0° Longitude.
In Africa: It crosses Ghana (Accra), Burkina Faso, Mali, and Algeria.

cases.title: Grid Reference
cases.description: Using Latitudes and Longitudes to find places.
steps: Grid Reference: The method of locating places using lines of Latitude and Longitude.
Intersection: The Equator and Prime Meridian meet in the Gulf of Guinea (Atlantic Ocean), just south of Ghana.
Uganda's Location: Uganda is crossed by the Equator and is East of the Prime Meridian.
